title: Christella patens
taxonomy: Christella patens is classified as a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae, and its genus Christella is part of the subfamily Thelypteridoideae. It is native to New Guinea. Christella patens is also known as a member of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. This species is further classified into the class Polypodiopsida, which is a group of vascular plants that have a leafy appearance. Christella patens is a relatively rare species found in the tropical regions of New Guinea.
uses: Christella patens is a species of fern that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. It is reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Christella patens for medicinal purposes. Some studies suggest that the plant may have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, making it a potential candidate for the development of new medicines.

intro: Christella patens, also known as the wall fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae.
